Wild (2014)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $37,880,356Details
International Box Office $14,580,187
Worldwide Box Office $52,460,543
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $6,858,582
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $2,716,429
Total $9,575,011
Further financial details...

Synopsis

With the dissolution of her marriage and the death of her mother, Cheryl Strayed has lost all hope. After years of reckless, destructive behavior, she makes a rash decision. With absolutely no experience, driven only by sheer determination, Cheryl hikes more than a thousand miles of the Pacific Crest Trail, alone.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$606,810 (1.6% of total gross)
Legs:7.03 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:72.2% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$15,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.5 times production budget)
Theater counts:21 opening theaters/1,361 max. theaters, 5.8 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $54,633,854

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: December 3rd, 2014 (Limited) by Fox Searchlight
International Releases: January 15th, 2015 (Wide) (Germany)
January 16th, 2015 (Wide) (Mexico)
January 16th, 2015 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
January 16th, 2015 (Wide) (France)
January 22nd, 2015 (Wide) (Australia)
Video Release: March 17th, 2015 by Fox Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for sexual content, nudity, drug use, and language.
(Rating bulletin 2323, 5/21/2014)
Running Time: 119 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Autobiographical, Intertitle, Same Role, Multiple Actors
Plot point: Addiction, Biography, Dysfunctional Family, Hitchhiker, Infidelity, Journey of Discovery, Narcotics, Non-Chronological, Relationships Gone Wrong, Sex Addiction
Social setting: Camping
Subgenre: Biographical Drama
Source:Based on Factual Book/Article
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: Pacific Standard
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

With the dissolution of her marriage and the death of her mother, Cheryl Strayed has lost all hope. After years of reckless, destructive behavior, she makes a rash decision. With absolutely no experience, driven only by sheer determination, Cheryl hikes more than a thousand miles of the Pacific Crest Trail, alone.
